preview_text:
The model favors England at 53% to win, with Croatia trailing at 18%. That 35-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial edge for the favorite, though the underdog retains a genuine chance at an upset.

The draw probability of 29% sits squarely between the two outcomes, suggesting this match is expected to be competitive and genuinely uncertain. The win probabilities are separated but not dramatically so; England's lead is real yet narrow enough that the result remains far from a foregone conclusion. A stalemate emerges as a plausible outcome given the spacing across the three scenarios.

The model and market are broadly aligned. The sportsbook consensus places England at 55% and the draw at 25%, within a few percentage points of the model's readings. Polymarket prices deviate markedly—all three outcomes priced at an even 33% each—suggesting either fundamentally different information or less refined forecasting from those traders. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources.
